unit.name: Generalized diffusion perspectives and autoregressive latents
unit.description: Surveys generalized diffusion choices, mathematical interpretations, and the return of autoregressive models on latents.
unit.summary: The final technical segment broadens from rectified flow to a generalized diffusion design space [ts=3841s]. Rectified flow is restated as sampling x, z, and t, setting xt as a linear interpolation, setting the ground-truth velocity vgt=z-x, predicting v from f_theta(xt,t), and minimizing an L2 loss [ts=3864s], [ts=3894s]. Other diffusion formulations vary the t distribution, the functions used to mix x and z into xt, and the target ygt that the network predicts [ts=3916s], [ts=3946s]. Examples include variance-preserving schedules, variance-exploding schedules, x-prediction, noise-prediction, and v-prediction [ts=3990s], [ts=4026s]. The lecturer then names three mathematical perspectives without deriving them: diffusion as a latent variable model similar to a VAE optimizing a variational lower bound [ts=4074s], diffusion as score-function learning where the score is the gradient of log pdata(x) pointing toward high-density regions [ts=4117s], and diffusion as solving stochastic differential equations with learned numerical integrators [ts=4176s]. The lecture closes by noting that autoregressive models also return in modern pipelines when trained over discrete latents from an encoder-decoder, so GANs, VAEs, autoregressive models, and diffusion all combine in contemporary generative systems [ts=4265s], [ts=4298s].

key_points.text: Rectified flow is one simple case where xt is linear interpolation and the target velocity is z-x.
Generalized diffusion varies p(t), the noisy-sample construction xt, and the target ygt.
Variance-preserving formulations choose mixing weights so unit-variance independent x and z keep unit variance.
Variance-exploding formulations set a(t)=1 and b(t)=sigma(t).
Targets may be x-prediction, noise-prediction, or v-prediction.
Diffusion can be viewed as a latent variable model optimized with a variational lower bound, similar to VAEs.
Diffusion can be viewed as learning the score function, the gradient of log p(x) pointing toward high-density regions.
Diffusion can be viewed as solving stochastic differential equations with learned numerical integrators.

kp_catalog.name: Generalized diffusion design space
kp_catalog.description: Different diffusion variants choose different p(t), noisy-sample functions, and prediction targets.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because generalized diffusion design space clarifies different diffusion variants choose different p(t), noisy-sample functions, and prediction targets, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Diffusion mathematical perspectives
kp_catalog.description: Diffusion can be understood as a latent-variable model, score-function learning, or solving SDEs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because diffusion mathematical perspectives extends the core thread to diffusion can be understood as a latent-variable model, score-function learning, or solving SDEs without being required for the main pipeline.

kp_catalog.name: Autoregressive models on discrete latents
kp_catalog.description: Modern autoregressive generators can model sequences of discrete latents from encoder-decoder systems.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because autoregressive models on discrete latents clarifies modern autoregressive generators can model sequences of discrete latents from encoder-decoder systems, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
